title: Tax Microdata Benchmarking Review
text: 1 Tax Microdata Benchmarking Review

title: Tax Microdata Benchmarking Review
text:
1.1 Overview
This document tracks development issues and solutions for the tax-microdata-benchmarking project.

title: Tax Microdata Benchmarking Review
text:
1.2 Recent Issues

1.2.1 SSL Certificate Verification Issue (#354)
Date: August 26, 2025
Status: Fixed via PR
Problem: - CPS data downloads were failing due to SSL certificate verification errors - Error occurred in tmd/datasets/cps.py line 176 - Census Bureau’s SSL configuration had compatibility issues with Python/requests/SSL setups
Solution: - Added verify=False to the requests.get() call in tmd/datasets/cps.py - This disables SSL verification for the trusted Census Bureau data source - Allows CPS data download process to complete successfully
Implementation:
# Before
response = requests.get(url, stream=True)

# After  
response = requests.get(url, stream=True, verify=False)


1.2.2 Tax-Calculator 5.2.0 Compatibility Issue
Date: August 26, 2025
Status: To be addressed in separate issue/PR
Problem: - Tests fail when using Tax-Calculator version 5.2.0 - Tests work correctly with Tax-Calculator version 5.1.0 - Unrelated to the SSL verification fix
Current Status: - SSL fix PR submitted with Tax-Calculator 5.2.0 as base - Tax-Calculator compatibility issue will be addressed separately after SSL fix is merged - Allows for independent review and resolution of each issue

title: Tax Microdata Benchmarking Review
text:
1.3 Contributing Guidelines

1.3.1 Repository Workflow
This project uses a trusted contributor model: - Contributors push branches directly to the upstream repository (PSLmodels/tax-microdata-benchmarking) - Do not create forks - push directly to upstream - Create pull requests from branches in the main repository


1.3.2 Authentication Setup
For SSH authentication with GitHub:

Generate SSH key:
ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -C "your-email@example.com" -f ~/.ssh/id_ed25519 -N ""
Add public key to GitHub:

Copy contents of ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub
Go to GitHub Settings → SSH and GPG keys → New SSH key
Paste key and save

Update remote to use SSH:
git remote set-url upstream git@github.com:PSLmodels/tax-microdata-benchmarking.git



1.3.3 Pull Request Process

Create feature branch from master
Make changes and commit
Push branch to upstream repository
Create pull request on GitHub
Allow 1-2 days for review
Address any feedback and merge


Last updated: August 26, 2025

title: 3  Project Description
text:
3.1 Tax Microdata Benchmarking
This repository develops a general-purpose validated microdata file for use in tax microsimulation models, specifically:

title: 3  Project Description
text:
PolicyEngine-US
Tax-Calculator

title: 3  Project Description
text:
3.2 Development Phases
The project progresses through multiple phases of refinement:

Phase 1: Base dataset from PolicyEngine-US PUF-enhanced CPS data
Phase 2-3: Iterative improvements and validation
Phase 4+/6: Current phase (version 1.0.0 released March 2025)

title: 3  Project Description
text:
3.3 Key Components

3.3.1 Data Sources

Licensed 2015 PUF data from IRS/SOI
Current Population Survey (CPS) data from Census Bureau
Various targeting data for validation



3.3.2 Outputs

Three TMD CSV files for Tax-Calculator usage
Comprehensive benchmarking results
Sub-national area weights for geographic analysis



3.3.3 Validation

Extensive comparison with federal agency estimates
Testing against CBO, JCT, and Treasury projections
Multi-year validation (2023, 2026)

title: 3  Project Description
text:
3.4 Technical Infrastructure

Python-based data processing pipeline
Comprehensive testing framework with pytest
Quarto-based documentation system
Git-based version control with trusted contributor model

title: 4  Questions and Answers
text: 4.1 Common Issues

title: 4  Questions and Answers
text:
4.1.1 Q: Why are CPS downloads failing with SSL errors?
A: The Census Bureau’s SSL certificate configuration has compatibility issues with certain Python/requests/SSL library combinations. This is resolved by adding verify=False to the requests call, which is safe since the Census Bureau is a trusted government data source.


4.1.2 Q: Why do tests fail with Tax-Calculator 5.2.0?
A: There are compatibility issues between the current TMD codebase and Tax-Calculator version 5.2.0. Tests pass with version 5.1.0. This is being addressed as a separate issue from the SSL fix to allow independent resolution.


4.1.3 Q: How do I contribute to this project?
A: This project uses a trusted contributor model: 1. Push branches directly to the upstream repository (don’t fork) 2. Create pull requests from upstream branches 3. Use SSH authentication for GitHub access 4. Allow 1-2 days for review before merging


4.1.4 Q: What’s the difference between the phases?
A: Each phase represents an iteration of dataset improvement: - Phase 1: Initial dataset based on PolicyEngine-US data - Phase 2-3: Refinements based on benchmarking results - Phase 4+: Current phase with comprehensive validation

title: 4  Questions and Answers
text:
4.2 Technical Questions

4.2.1 Q: How do I set up the development environment?
A: 1. Clone the repository 2. Install dependencies: pip install -e . 3. License and obtain 2015 PUF data from IRS/SOI 4. Place PUF files in tmd/storage/input/ 5. Run make data to generate TMD files


4.2.2 Q: How do I run the tests?
A: Use pytest: pytest tests/ (Note: currently requires Tax-Calculator 5.1.0)


4.2.3 Q: Where are the examination results?
A: See the tmd/examination/ directory for detailed benchmarking results comparing TMD estimates with federal agency projections.

title: 5  Change Log
text:
5.1.1 SSL Certificate Verification Fix

Issue: CPS data downloads failing due to SSL verification errors
Fix: Added verify=False to requests.get() call in tmd/datasets/cps.py
Status: PR submitted for review
Impact: Resolves data generation pipeline blockage



5.1.2 Tax-Calculator Version Issue Identified

Issue: Tests fail with Tax-Calculator 5.2.0, pass with 5.1.0
Status: Deferred to separate issue/PR after SSL fix
Impact: Temporary test failures, does not affect SSL fix functionality



5.1.3 Development Environment Updates

SSH Authentication: Set up SSH keys for GitHub access
Repository Access: Confirmed trusted contributor model workflow
Documentation: Created comprehensive Quarto project documentation



5.1.4 Process Improvements

Separation of Concerns: Addressing SSL fix independently from Tax-Calculator compatibility
Documentation: Established clear issue tracking and resolution process
Contributing Guidelines: Documented trusted contributor workflow
